subtitle: Massacre the Guinea-Pigs
summary: Roux and Yersin set out to prove that the diphtheria bacillus kills by releasing a poison (toxin) into the body. After filtering living germs out of broth, they struggled for weeks to show the germ-free liquid was deadly. When sensible doses failed, Roux injected a near-drowning quantity into a guinea-pig — and it died with classic diphtheria symptoms. He then learned to let the germs brew longer, producing a toxin so concentrated that one ounce could kill six hundred thousand guinea-pigs. Roux had proven Loeffler's prophecy true, but he still had no cure.

segments.anchor_sentence: Four years later Loeffler's words came true—by what seemed an utterly silly, but what was surely a most fantastical experiment you would have thought could only result in drowning a guinea-pig.

visual.scene_description: The bustling Pasteur Institute on Rue Dutot in the late 1880s, with Roux reading a mother's desperate letter while an aging Pasteur oversees construction in the background.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, gaunt dark-bearded man in rolled-up shirtsleeves reading a handwritten letter at a cluttered desk, behind him a half-built stone institute on a Paris street, an elderly bearded man with a cane supervising workers, glass flasks and microscopes on shelves, gaslight sconces on walls

segments.anchor_sentence: Roux and Yersin went to the Hospital for Sick Children—diphtheria was playing hell with Paris—and here they ran on to the same bacillus Loeffler had found.

visual.scene_description: Roux and Yersin in their laboratory examining dead rabbits, searching their tissues for bacilli but finding none, realizing the germs must produce a poison.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, gaunt bearded man and younger assistant dissecting a rabbit on a wooden laboratory bench, glass flasks of cloudy broth nearby, brass compound microscope, circular magnified inset showing rod-shaped diphtheria bacilli with club-shaped ends among empty tissue, gaslit Paris laboratory with bottle-loaded shelves

segments.anchor_sentence: For a while the searcher came uppermost in him.

visual.scene_description: Roux carefully filtering diphtheria broth through a porcelain candle filter, collecting clear amber fluid into small flasks, then injecting it into guinea-pigs.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, gaunt dark-bearded man in rolled-up shirtsleeves pressing cloudy broth through a hollow porcelain candle filter mounted in a glass cylinder using air pressure apparatus, clear amber fluid dripping into small glass flasks on a laboratory bench, large-bellied bottles in background, syringe beside a docile guinea-pig in a cage

segments.anchor_sentence: He became a murderer in his heart, this Émile Roux, and in his head as he came down to the laboratory each morning were half-mad wishes for the death of his beasts.

visual.scene_description: Roux frustrated in his lab as the guinea-pigs remain perfectly healthy after injection, hopping about in their cages while he stares at them in disbelief.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, gaunt hawk-faced bearded man slumped on a stool staring at wire cages full of lively guinea-pigs eating greens and hopping, empty syringes and glass flasks of amber liquid on the bench beside him, dusty bottle-loaded shelves lining the walls of a dim Paris laboratory

segments.anchor_sentence: Then—I have told scientific searchers about this and they have held their noses at such an experiment—Roux nearly drowned a guinea-pig.

visual.scene_description: Roux injecting an enormous thirty-five cubic centimeter dose of filtered broth into a guinea-pig — a desperately oversized dose that ultimately proves the toxin exists when the animal dies days later.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, gaunt dark-bearded man with intense expression carefully injecting an enormous glass syringe full of amber liquid into a small guinea-pig held on a laboratory bench, rows of glass bottles and flasks behind him, circular magnified inset showing tiny rod-shaped diphtheria bacilli releasing clouds of dark toxin droplets into surrounding fluid

segments.anchor_sentence: But Roux had got his start.

visual.scene_description: Roux triumphant in his laboratory with rows of large bottles that have brewed for forty-two days, watching tiny drops of ultra-concentrated toxin prove devastatingly potent.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, gaunt dark-bearded man with bright determined eyes holding up a tiny glass vial of concentrated amber toxin, rows of large-bellied glass bottles in a warm incubator behind him, a precise laboratory scale and notebook on the bench, circular magnified inset showing rod-shaped bacilli shedding dark toxin granules from their cell walls
